meaning: vt.集聚,集中,施行中央集权vi.集中于中央(或中心)
examples: In the mass production era, multinational firms tended to centralize their operations.
In order to raise the efficiency of the water supply, measures should be taken to centralize the management of water resources.
Centralize business processes across business units.
That would make the NBA the first major sports league to largely de-centralize its Web operation.
The relocation allowed Christie's to centralize its legion of New York-based users on one LAN.
